question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed if clear
Come to a complete stop, then go when safe
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Yield to traffic on the right
explanation: An octagon always means STOP. You must come to a complete stop at the stop line or crosswalk, then proceed only when the way is clear.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25-27 — Traffic Control Signs

question: What does this sign tell you to do?
choices: Slow down and give the right-of-way to crossing or merging traffic
Speed up to merge with traffic
Make no turns at this intersection
explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a YIELD sign: slow down, be ready to stop, and let traffic, pedestrians, or bicycles pass before you proceed.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25-27 — Traffic Control Signs

question: Which shape is used ONLY for stop signs?
explanation: The 8-sided octagon is reserved exclusively for stop signs so drivers can recognize it by shape alone, even if the face is covered with snow.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25 — Stop Signs

question: A diamond-shaped yellow sign means:
choices: A warning about a hazard or change in the road ahead
explanation: Yellow diamond signs warn of hazards ahead: curves, intersections, merges, narrow bridges, and similar conditions. Slow down and be ready to react.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Warning)

question: What does this sign warn you about?
choices: The road ahead curves right, then left
The road ahead curves left, then right
A winding road for the next several miles
explanation: The arrow shows the path of the road: it bends right first, then back to the left. Slow down before entering the curves.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Warning)

question: What does this round yellow sign with an X and 'RR' mean?
choices: Railroad crossing ahead — slow down and be ready to stop
You are at the railroad tracks — stop immediately
Intersection with a one-way street ahead
explanation: The round advance warning sign is posted BEFORE a railroad crossing. Slow down, look and listen for trains, and be prepared to stop before the tracks.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 28 — Railroad Crossing Signs

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: You are entering a school zone or near a school crossing
Pedestrian crosswalk at a shopping area
explanation: The 5-sided (pentagon) sign marks a school zone or school crossing. Slow down, obey the posted school speed limit, and watch for children.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (School Zone)

question: This sideways triangle (pennant) sign on the LEFT side of the road means:
choices: Do not pass — you are entering a no-passing zone
explanation: The pennant-shaped NO PASSING ZONE sign is the only sign posted on the left side of the road. It marks where passing is prohibited.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 28, 62 — No Passing Zone

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: One-way street — do not drive in
Do not enter — the roadway ahead is closed to you
explanation: The red circle with the white bar means DO NOT ENTER. It is posted at exit ramps and one-way streets to keep you from driving into oncoming traffic.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25-27 — Traffic Control Signs

question: If you see this sign facing you, what has happened?
choices: You are driving against traffic on a one-way road or ramp
The road is closed for construction
You are in a truck-only lane
explanation: WRONG WAY means you have entered a roadway against the flow of traffic. Pull over and turn around as soon as it is safe.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25-27 — Traffic Control Signs

question: Orange signs and barrels mean:
choices: Construction or maintenance work area ahead
explanation: Orange is the color of work zones. Slow down, obey the posted work-zone speed limit, and watch for workers and equipment. Fines often double in work zones.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 16-21 — Work Zone Safety

question: What does this warning sign mean?
choices: Lane ends — traffic from the right is merging into your road
explanation: The merge sign warns that another roadway joins yours ahead. Adjust your speed and position to let merging traffic blend in safely.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Warning)

question: An orange triangle on the back of a vehicle means it is:
choices: A slow-moving vehicle traveling 25 mph or less
explanation: The orange-and-red triangle marks slow-moving vehicles such as farm equipment. Slow down early and pass only when it is safe and legal.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 26 — Slow Moving Vehicle Sign

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
explanation: ONE WAY means all traffic on that street moves in the direction the arrow points. Never turn against the arrow.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25-27 — Traffic Control Signs

question: A solid yellow line on YOUR side of the center line means:
choices: Passing is allowed if you are careful
Do not pass — stay in your lane
The lane is for left turns only
Traffic moves in your direction on both sides
explanation: Yellow lines separate traffic moving in opposite directions. A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited for you. A broken line means you may pass when safe.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Pavement Markings

question: White lane lines separate:
choices: Traffic moving in the same direction
explanation: White lines divide lanes of traffic moving in the SAME direction; yellow lines divide traffic moving in OPPOSITE directions.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Pavement Markings

question: A flashing red traffic light means:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Treat it like a stop sign: stop completely, then go when safe
The signal is broken — proceed normally
Stop and wait until the light stops flashing
explanation: Flashing red = stop sign. Stop completely, yield, then proceed when safe. Flashing yellow means slow down and proceed with caution.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 23-24 — Lighted Traffic Signals

question: A steady green light means:
choices: Go immediately — you always have the right-of-way
Go, but first yield to vehicles and pedestrians still in the intersection
Speed up before the light changes
explanation: Green means you may go, but only after yielding to pedestrians and any vehicles still clearing the intersection.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 23-24 — Lighted Traffic Signals

question: A large X with the letters 'RR' is painted on the pavement of your lane. This means:
choices: The same thing as the round advance warning sign — a railroad crossing is ahead
explanation: Railroad pavement markings mean the same as the advance warning sign: slow down, look and listen, and be prepared to stop. On two-lane roads they come with a no-passing marking, and there may be a white stop line before the tracks.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 28 — Railroad Pavement Markings

question: What does this sign set?
choices: The minimum speed you must travel
The maximum legal speed under ideal conditions
explanation: A speed limit sign sets the MAXIMUM legal speed in ideal conditions. In rain, fog, or heavy traffic, the safe speed may be much lower.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25 — Speed Limit Sign

question: A steady RED arrow on a traffic light means:
choices: Turn quickly before cross traffic starts
Stop — you may not turn in the direction of the arrow
explanation: A steady red arrow means stop: turning in the arrow's direction is not allowed until the arrow changes.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 24 — Lighted Arrows

question: A FLASHING yellow arrow means:
choices: Left turns are protected — oncoming traffic must stop
Left turns are permitted, but you must yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians first
The signal is about to turn green
explanation: Flashing yellow arrow = turns permitted but NOT protected: yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians, then proceed with caution. A steady GREEN arrow is the protected turn.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 24 — Lighted Arrows

question: A GREEN arrow on a traffic light means:
choices: You may turn in the direction of the arrow — the turn is protected and opposing traffic must stop
Yield to oncoming traffic before turning
explanation: A green arrow is a protected turn: opposing traffic is stopped. Still check that the intersection is clear before turning.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 24 — Lighted Arrows

question: A steady YELLOW traffic light means:
choices: Speed up before it turns red
The signal is changing from green to red — stop if it is safe to do so
explanation: Steady yellow = the light is about to turn red. Stop if you can do so safely. If you are already stopped at the line, you may not proceed.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 23 — Lighted Traffic Signals

question: Before turning right at a red light, you must:
choices: Come to a complete stop, check for traffic and pedestrians, and make sure no sign prohibits the turn
Wait for green — right on red is illegal in Arkansas
explanation: Right on red is legal in Arkansas after a complete stop and a check for opposing traffic and pedestrians — unless a sign says NO RIGHT TURN ON RED. Never stop or block a crosswalk.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 25 — Right on Red Rule

question: The white X-shaped CROSSBUCK sign at railroad tracks requires you to:
choices: Yield the right-of-way to the train
explanation: The crossbuck marks the crossing itself and works like a yield sign: yield the right-of-way to any train. Failing to yield at a yield sign can mean a fine up to $1,000 and a 180-day license suspension.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 28 — Cross Buck Sign

question: An EXEMPT sign below a railroad crossbuck means:
choices: The crossing has been abandoned or its use discontinued
Trains do not blow horns here
School buses may cross without stopping
explanation: EXEMPT marks a crossing that has been abandoned or taken out of service. Follow your local procedure about stopping.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 29 — Railroad (Exempt) Signs

question: A small sign under a railroad crossbuck reads '2 TRACKS'. This warns you:
choices: Two trains per day use this crossing
There is more than one track — after one train passes, another may be coming from either direction
The crossing is two miles long
Trucks must use the second track crossing
explanation: The number sign tells how many tracks are present. Cross multiple tracks only when any train is well down the tracks — a second train can be hidden behind the first.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 30 — Multiple Tracks

question: A curb painted RED means:
explanation: Red curb = fire zone. Yellow curb = loading zone or other restriction. Always check for signs that limit parking.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 42 — Restricted Parking Zones

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are prohibited at this intersection
explanation: A red circle-and-slash over a symbol prohibits that maneuver. This one bans left turns; the same pattern is used for no right turn and no U-turn.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Regulation Signs

question: The solid white line along the right edge of the roadway (the 'fog line') shows:
choices: Where the outer edge of the road is located
explanation: The fog line marks the outer edge of the roadway — especially useful at night and in rain or fog. Never drive on the shoulder beyond it unless directed by police.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Fog Line and Lane Lines

question: The center of a two-way road has a solid yellow line next to a broken yellow line. Passing is permitted:
choices: For traffic on the side with the broken line, when there is no opposing traffic
For traffic on the side with the solid line
explanation: Passing is permitted on the side with the BROKEN line and prohibited on the side with the solid line. A double solid yellow line means no passing either direction.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Yellow Lane Markings

question: In a work zone, a flagger holds a STOP/SLOW paddle. The flagger's instructions:
choices: Are suggestions — the posted signs control
Overrule any other traffic signals or devices
Apply only when workers are present
explanation: Flaggers are legally authorized to direct traffic in work zones — their signals overrule other traffic signals and devices. Stop at the designated spot or at least a car length away.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 22 — Work Zone Flaggers

question: What does this sign warn you about?
choices: An intersection or cross road ahead
explanation: The yellow diamond with a cross symbol warns of an intersection ahead — traffic may merge or cross. Slow down and look left and right.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Intersection)

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your traffic lane ends ahead — merge into the remaining lane
explanation: The lane-ends warning sign tells you a lane is ending. Merge early and smoothly — don't race to the end of the closing lane.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Traffic Lane Ends)

question: What should you do when you see this sign?
choices: Slow down and be prepared to stop — a traffic signal is ahead
Speed up to make the light
Turn around; the road is closed
explanation: The signal-ahead warning sign is posted where a traffic light may not be visible in time — often before hills or curves. Be prepared to stop.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Traffic Signal)

question: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger may be stationed ahead to control traffic — obey their directions
explanation: The flagger-symbol sign marks locations where a flagger may be controlling traffic. A flagger's instructions overrule all other signals and devices.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 18 — Work Zone Signs

question: An orange DETOUR sign warns you of:
choices: An approaching alternate route of travel through a construction zone
The end of the work zone
explanation: DETOUR signs warn drivers of an approaching alternate route through a construction zone. ROAD WORK signs with distances mean work is underway on your route — be prepared for obstructions.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 18 — Work Zone Signs

question: What does this sign warn you about?
choices: Two-way traffic along a single roadway
explanation: The two-way traffic sign warns that opposing traffic shares the roadway — in work zones, often because one side of a divided highway is closed. Stay alert and keep right.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 18 — Work Zone Signs

question: You see FLASHING GREEN lights on a vehicle along the roadway. In Arkansas this indicates:
choices: An active work zone — a construction or maintenance vehicle is working; use caution and slow down
explanation: Arkansas recently authorized flashing green lights (along with yellow or white) on construction and maintenance vehicles. They mark an active standard or mobile work zone.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 18 — Green Lights in Work Zones

question: An ARDOT truck ahead displays a sign on its back: 'WORK ZONE — FINES DOUBLE'. This means you are approaching:
choices: A mobile work zone — a crew working at slow speed with periodic stops, where fines double like any work zone
explanation: Mobile work zones (striping crews, pothole patching, debris removal) are marked by signs on the back of ARDOT vehicles. Fines double there just like in standard work zones.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 17-19 — Mobile Work Zones

question: Work zone signs may be diamond or rectangular shaped, with a background that is:
explanation: Work zone signs use orange, white, or black backgrounds with white or black letters or symbols — covering reduced speeds, detours, slow equipment, and changing surfaces.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 18 — Work Zone Signs

question: The blue ENS sign posted at every railroad crossing gives you:
choices: An emergency phone number and the crossing's ID number, so you can report a problem (like a stalled car) to the railroad
explanation: The Emergency Notification System sign lets the public reach the railroad responsible for the crossing and identify the exact crossing in an emergency — critical if a vehicle is stuck on the tracks.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 29 — Emergency Notification Systems

question: What does this sign warn you about?
choices: The road is slippery when wet — slow down in rain
explanation: The slippery-when-wet warning marks pavement that loses traction in rain. Slow down at the first sign of rain — especially the first few minutes, when surface oil hasn't washed away.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (Slippery When Wet)

question: A five-sided school sign showing children with crosswalk lines beneath them marks:
choices: A school CROSSING — a place where children cross the road
explanation: The pentagon with crosswalk lines marks the school crossing itself; without the lines it marks the school zone. Either way: slow down and watch for children.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Roadway Signs (School Crossing)

question: There is a white stop line painted across your lane at a stop sign. Where must you stop?
choices: With your front bumper past the line
Before the vehicle reaches the stop line
explanation: Make a complete stop BEFORE reaching the stop line. If a sign or obstacle blocks your view, stop first, then pull forward carefully until you can see.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 34 — Crosswalks and Stop Lines

question: What does this sign warn you about?
choices: A pedestrian crossing ahead — be ready to slow or stop for people crossing
explanation: The pedestrian-crossing warning diamond marks places where people frequently cross. Yield to pedestrians in or about to enter the crosswalk — and remember not all crosswalks are marked.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27, 34 — Roadway Signs (Pedestrian Crossing)

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: The red circle-and-slash over the U-shaped arrow prohibits U-turns. Even where no sign prohibits them, only make a U-turn where you can see clearly in both directions.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Regulation Signs (No U-Turn)

question: A steady YELLOW arrow at a traffic signal means:
choices: Left turns are protected — go ahead
The signal is about to turn red; a driver turning should stop if it is safe to do so
Turns are permitted but you must yield to oncoming traffic
Stop and do not turn at all
explanation: A steady yellow arrow means the signal is getting ready to turn red. A driver turning should stop if it is safe to do so — it works like a steady yellow light, but for the turn movement.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 24 — Lighted Arrows

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: The red circle-and-slash over the right-turn arrow prohibits right turns at this location. Regulation signs use a red circle with a slash to show that a maneuver is not allowed.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Regulation Signs

question: On a two-lane road you see a solid yellow line right next to a broken yellow line. Passing is permitted:
choices: Only from the side where the line is BROKEN
Only from the side where the line is SOLID
explanation: You may pass only from the side where the line is broken. If the solid yellow line is on your side, passing is not permitted.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Yellow Lane Markings

question: You want to turn left into a driveway, but a solid double yellow line runs down the center of the road. You may:
choices: Never cross a double yellow line for any reason
Cross the double yellow line to turn into the driveway if no opposing traffic is approaching
Cross it only at an intersection
Cross it only after signaling for at least 5 seconds
explanation: Double solid yellow lines prohibit passing in both directions, but a driver MAY cross them to turn into a driveway or alley when no opposing traffic is approaching.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Yellow Lane Markings

question: You have started across railroad tracks and the crossing gates begin to come down. You should:
choices: Stop on the tracks and wait
Back up off the crossing quickly
Keep going and clear the tracks, even if it means breaking the gate
Stop and put the car in reverse
explanation: Never drive around lowered gates. But if the gates begin to lower while you are already crossing, keep going and clear the tracks — even if it means breaking the gate. Never stop on the tracks.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 29 — Gates and Lights

question: At a railroad crossing the red lights are flashing but you do not see a train. You should:
choices: Proceed across since no train is visible
Stop — a train is present or approaching; do not cross even if the lights keep flashing and no train appears
Speed across before the gates lower
Treat the flashing lights like a yield sign
explanation: Flashing red lights mean a train is present or approaching. Stop and do not cross. Even if the lights continue to flash and no train appears, do not cross.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 29 — Gates and Lights

question: A white square or rectangular sign showing a symbol inside a red circle with a red slash through it tells a driver that:
choices: The maneuver shown is prohibited (not allowed)
explanation: Regulation signs are white squares or rectangles with black and/or red letters or symbols. A red circle with a slash over a symbol means that maneuver — such as a turn — is prohibited.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 27 — Regulation Signs

question: A sign below the crossbuck at a railroad crossing reads '2 TRACKS.' After the first train passes, you should:
choices: Watch for additional trains from either direction and cross only when the train is well down the tracks
Assume no other train is coming
explanation: A number beneath the crossbuck tells how many tracks are present. Watch for additional trains coming from either direction, and cross only when the train is well down the tracks.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 30 — Multiple Tracks

question: A FLASHING yellow traffic light means:
choices: Stop, then go when safe — like a stop sign
Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is broken; treat it as green
Make a complete stop and wait
explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed with caution. (A flashing RED light means stop, then go when it's safe, just like a stop sign.)
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 24 — Flashing Lights

question: What is the difference between WHITE and YELLOW lines painted on the road?
choices: White lines separate traffic going the SAME direction; yellow lines separate traffic going OPPOSITE directions
Yellow lines mark same-direction lanes; white lines mark opposite directions
White lines are only for parking
explanation: White lane lines separate lanes of traffic moving in the same direction. Yellow lines separate traffic moving in opposite directions (and mark the left edge of one-way roads).
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 31 — Pavement Markings

question: You see ORANGE signs and cones along the road. These tell you that:
choices: You are in or approaching a construction or maintenance work zone — slow down and be prepared for changes
explanation: Orange is the color for temporary traffic control in work zones. Orange signs, cones, and barrels mark construction and maintenance areas — reduce speed and watch for workers and changing conditions.
citation: AR Driver License Study Guide, p. 16, 18 — Work Zone Safety / Signs
